title: Fiery Middle Eastern Fish Shakshuka
description: A spicy and silky one-pot Middle Eastern dish featuring sweet chilli sauce and nuka-pickle, served in a slate-tile vessel.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Heat the olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Stir in the sweet chilli sauce, nuka-pickle, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 2 minutes.
Nestle the cod fillets into the sauce, making sure they are covered.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 12 minutes.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Serve the fish and sauce in the slate-tile vessel, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
